title: cerastium arvense subsp. pallasii
intro: Cerastium arvense subsp. pallasii, commonly known as Pallas's chickweed or Russian chickweed, is a plant species in the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae) that is native to Russia, Eastern Europe, and Central Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful in treating wounds, skin conditions, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Cerastium arvense subsp. pallasii include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made by infusing the leaves and stems in hot water, and it can also be used topically as a salve or ointment.
